data.title: Maersk keeps full-year outlook amid trade hurdles, global uncertainty
data.description:
A.P. Moller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) reaffirmed its full-year guidance on Thursday, even as it acknowledged increasing volatility in global container shipping because of economic and geopolitical tensions -- particularly between the United States and China.

The Danish shipping giant cited U.S. President Donald Trump's latest wave of tariffs, which now amount to 145% on Chinese imports, as a major source of disruption. Chief Executive Vincent Clerc told CNBC that container volumes between the U.S. and China have fallen by 30% to 40% since April. In response, Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) has reallocated some of its larger vessels from trans-Pacific routes to other lanes with more stable demand.

While trade between other regions has so far remained relatively unaffected, Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) warned that escalating protectionist policies could ripple across global markets. The company noted that if Chinese exporters redirect shipments originally bound for the U.S. to other regions, it could spark retaliatory measures and further escalate trade tensions.

Despite these challenges, Maersk's (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) core shipping division saw strong performance in the first quarter. Freight revenues rose 13%, supported by a 2.5% year-over-year increase in rates and steady shipping volumes. Operational costs were kept in check thanks to ongoing efficiency initiatives and a 9% drop in fuel prices.

"With trade tensions flaring up and uncertainty on the rise, global supply chains are once again in the spotlight," Clerc said in a statement. "We are doubling down on the work underway on automation and cost management to remain fit for what lies ahead."

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) reported first-quarter revenue of $13.32 billion, up from $12.36 billion a year ago. Underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization climbed to $2.71 billion from $1.6 billion, and underlying ebit jumped to $1.2 billion from $174 million. These results slightly outpaced analyst expectations, according to a FactSet poll cited by The Wall Street Journal. The consensus forecasted $13.26 billion in revenue, $2.63 billion in ebitda, and $1.15 billion in ebit.

The company continues to project full-year underlying ebitda of $6 billion to $9 billion and ebit of up to $3 billion. However, Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) revised its global container volume forecast to a range of -1% to +4%, down from its earlier projection of approximately 4% growth for 2025.

Looking ahead, Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKAF) (OTCPK:AMKBF) flagged persistent uncertainty in the global trade environment, warning that U.S. recession risks and an unstable trade policy landscape could dampen demand. The company also expects Red Sea shipping disruptions to persist through the remainder of the year.

data.title: Dentsply sirona sees 2025 revenue at $3.6B-$3.7B with expanded EBITDA margin guidance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Dentsply Sirona (XRAY) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Simon Campion highlighted that organic sales in two of three global regions grew, with imaging and the Wellspect Healthcare segment showing strong performance. Europe reported its second consecutive quarter of growth, and Germany delivered growth for the third consecutive quarter.
  • Management noted the success of the company's efforts to reduce operating expenditure, which contributed to EBITDA margin expansion in the quarter.
  • The company’s DS Core ecosystem has gained traction, now boasting over 42,000 unique users and 50,000 connected devices. Enhancements to the platform, such as DS Core Diagnose and Primescan 2 functionalities, aim to drive better patient engagement and improved workflows.
  • CFO remarks emphasized adjusted EPS growth, driven by margin expansion and operational efficiencies, and noted the financial discipline applied through transformation initiatives.

Outlook

  • The company maintained its 2025 organic sales guidance, projecting a decline of 2%-4%, with Byte contributing a 2% negative impact. Reported sales guidance was updated to $3.6 billion-$3.7 billion, reflecting changes in foreign currency rates.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance for 2025 was raised to exceed 19%. Adjusted EPS is expected to remain in the range of $1.80 to $2.00.
  • Management anticipates mid-single-digit organic sales declines for Q2 2025, primarily driven by Byte and seasonal factors.

Financial Results

  • First quarter revenue totaled $879 million, a decline of 7.7% year-over-year, with a 4.4% decline on an organic basis.
  • Adjusted gross margin remained flat, while adjusted EBITDA margins expanded by 220 basis points due to cost controls and operational efficiencies.
  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 2025 was $0.43, a 3.7% increase, supported by margin improvements and a lower share count.
  • Operating cash flow stood at $7 million, compared to $25 million in the prior year, primarily due to timing of cash collections and inventory build-up.

Q&A

  • Elizabeth Anderson, Evercore ISI: Questioned the tariff impact and CFO search. CEO Campion elaborated on the $0.10 EPS impact and annualized $50 million exposure from tariffs, with mitigation strategies being explored. The CFO search is progressing, with candidates in advanced stages.
  • Michael Cherny, Leerink Partners: Asked about orthodontic operations beyond GP markets. CEO Campion emphasized plans to reengage with the orthodontist community and improve the SureSmile user interface.
  • Dylan Finley, UBS: Inquired about EBITDA margin sustainability. CEO Campion indicated that Q1 performance is indicative of future margins and highlighted judicious SG&A management.
  • David Saxon, Needham & Company: Sought updates on implant performance. Management acknowledged disappointing results, outlining ongoing retraining and strategic adjustments to rebuild relationships with implant customers.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts seemed focused on operational execution and the impacts of broader market conditions, showing a slightly skeptical tone, particularly around tariffs and orthodontic strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks but adopted a more cautious and pragmatic approach during Q&A, especially when discussing tariffs and orthodontic market reengagement.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was consistent, emphasizing transformation and operational discipline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance remained unchanged for organic sales and adjusted EPS, though the reported sales range was updated for currency impacts.
  • Strategic focus continues on cost management, digital ecosystem expansion, and addressing underperformance in implants and orthodontics.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted slightly more towards understanding the implications of tariffs and strategic changes in orthodontics compared to the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariffs were noted as a potential risk, with current exposure estimated at $50 million annually. Mitigation strategies include stock adjustments and strategic pricing changes.
  • Orthodontic and implant segments remain areas of focus, with management acknowledging ongoing challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic conditions and their impact on U.S. dental markets, particularly in capital equipment and elective procedures.

Final Takeaway

Dentsply Sirona’s Q1 2025 results reflected operational efficiency gains and growth in key segments such as imaging and Wellspect. While maintaining a stable outlook on organic sales and earnings, the company adjusted reported sales guidance upwards. Management is actively addressing challenges in orthodontic and implant markets, with strategic initiatives focused on digital integration and improving customer engagement. Investors should monitor the company’s progress in mitigating tariff impacts and advancing its digital ecosystem, as these could significantly influence long-term performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Granite Real Estate Investment reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Granite Real Estate Investment press release (NYSE:GRP.UN): Q1 Granite's net operating income ("NOI") was $125.7 million in the first quarter of 2025 compared to $114.5 million in the prior year period.
  • Same property NOI - cash basis increased by 4.7% for the first quarter of 2025, excluding the impact of foreign exchange;
  • Funds from operations ("FFO")(1) was $91.0 million ($1.46 per unit) in the first quarter of 2025 compared to $82.4 million ($1.30 per unit) in the first quarter of 2024;
  • Adjusted funds from operations ("AFFO")(2) was $88.4 million ($1.41 per unit) in the first quarter of 2025 compared to $77.9 million ($1.22 per unit) in the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: Rheinmetall in deal with Finland's Iceye to launch military satellite venture
data.description:
Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) is teaming up with Finnish satellite operator Iceye to create a new joint venture focused on manufacturing military-grade satellites, as part of its broader strategy to redirect automotive production facilities toward defense-related operations.

The German defense firm announced on Thursday that it signed a memorandum of understanding with Iceye to establish Rheinmetall Iceye Space Solutions. Under the proposed structure, Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) will hold a 60% majority interest, with Iceye retaining the remaining 40%. Final agreements and regulatory approvals are still in progress.

The venture aims to produce synthetic aperture radar satellites, which are capable of capturing detailed images regardless of weather conditions or time of day. Manufacturing is expected to take place at several locations, including a Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) car parts plant in Neuss, a city in western Germany.

This move reflects Rheinmetall’s (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) ongoing initiative to repurpose automotive industry assets—especially those tied to Germany’s declining car manufacturing base—for defense production. The shift comes as European nations ramp up military spending in response to heightened tensions with Russia.

Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY), already Europe’s largest producer of ammunition, also reiterated earlier on Thursday that it may exceed its 2025 financial targets due to rising demand for defense equipment from Germany, Ukraine, and other European countries.
